provenance.review_note: Adult DKA/HHS tree is disease-specific and threshold-cited; local insulin infusion order sets, electrolyte replacement policy, ICU criteria, and SGLT2 restart decisions require clinician governance.

exact_data_needed: Known diabetes type, duration, insulin regimen, pump/CGM use, and last insulin dose?
Recent glucose values, beta-hydroxybutyrate/urine ketones, anion gap, bicarbonate, pH, potassium, creatinine, and osmolality if known?
Missed or reduced insulin, pump/infusion-set failure, empty reservoir, expired insulin, medication access issue, or recent dose change?
Polyuria, polydipsia, weight loss, dehydration, or very dry mouth?
focused exam findings listed for this workup
weight when dosing depends on it
Altered mental status, confusion, obtundation, seizure, or coma
Hypotension, shock, severe dehydration, poor perfusion, or inability to tolerate PO
Blood beta-hydroxybutyrate preferred; urine ketones only if blood ketones are unavailable
Basic metabolic panel/electrolytes with sodium, potassium, chloride, bicarbonate, BUN, creatinine, glucose, anion gap, and corrected sodium
Venous pH/blood gas for acidosis severity; arterial gas only when oxygenation/ventilation concern or institutional protocol requires it
Calculate effective osmolality and measure serum osmolality when HHS/mixed crisis, severe dehydration, altered mental status, or very high glucose is possible
ECG and potassium-focused monitoring if potassium abnormality, weakness, renal failure, or insulin therapy is expected
urine ketones if blood ketones unavailable
imaging, ECG, culture, pathology, or procedure result required by the chosen branch
Basic metabolic panel/electrolytes with sodium, potassium, chloride, bicarbonate, BUN, creatinine, glucose, anion gap, and corrected sodium
Calculate effective osmolality and measure serum osmolality when HHS/mixed crisis, severe dehydration, altered mental status, or very high glucose is possible
ECG and potassium-focused monitoring if potassium abnormality, weakness, renal failure, or insulin therapy is expected
CBC with differential plus magnesium and phosphate when crisis is suspected or insulin therapy is expected
Precipitant workup guided by presentation: urinalysis/culture, blood cultures, chest imaging, viral testing, pregnancy test, lipase, troponin/ECG, neuroimaging, or other source evaluation
pregnant/not pregnant/unknown when pregnancy potential exists
postpartum/lactation status if testing or treatment could change
fertility intent when treatment depends on it

DKA: diabetes/prior diabetes or glucose >=200 mg/dL, beta-hydroxybutyrate >=3.0 mmol/L or urine ketones >=2+, and pH <7.3 and/or bicarbonate <18 mmol/L; can be euglycemic with SGLT2, pregnancy, fasting, or poor intake
HHS: glucose >=600 mg/dL, effective osmolality >300 mOsm/kg or total osmolality >320 mOsm/kg, absent significant ketonemia, and no significant acidosis; assess for mixed DKA/HHS
Precipitating illness or barrier: infection, insulin omission/access issue, pump failure, MI/stroke, pancreatitis, glucocorticoids/other medications, pregnancy, alcohol, or other physiologic stress

Hyperglycemia without crisis: persistent inpatient glucose >=180 mg/dL generally needs insulin initiation/intensification, while mild stable T2DM may be managed less intensively per inpatient protocol
Suspected DKA/HHS is not routine outpatient hyperglycemia; use urgent evaluation with protocolized fluids, insulin, electrolyte replacement, and precipitant treatment.
ICU/high-acuity care is appropriate for severe DKA, HHS, shock, altered mental status, critical precipitating illness, severe electrolyte disturbance, or inability to monitor safely.

Uncomplicated mild/moderate DKA may be managed in ED observation or step-down with close nursing, serial labs, and IV or protocolized subcutaneous rapid-acting insulin; severe/complicated DKA and HHS need higher acuity.
Do not start or continue insulin infusion without potassium-aware protocol monitoring; if K <3.5 mmol/L, replace potassium and delay insulin until K is >3.5 mmol/L.

root.description: Activate for adult hyperglycemia, suspected DKA/HHS, ketones, acidosis, hyperosmolality, altered mental status, dehydration, SGLT2 inhibitor exposure, pregnancy, or clinician-selected hyperglycemic-crisis workup.

endpoint_missing_context.description: Use this branch when adult hyperglycemic-crisis pathway activation cannot be determined from extractable patient context.

adult_dka_hhs_initial_assessment.description: Initial adult DKA/HHS assessment requires concurrent clinical severity and lab data before a non-emergency branch is selected.

criteria_options.label: Adult DKA: diabetes/prior diabetes or glucose >=200 mg/dL, beta-hydroxybutyrate >=3.0 mmol/L or urine ketones >=2+, and pH <7.3 and/or bicarbonate <18 mmol/L
criteria_options.criteria_text: Classify DKA when diabetes/prior diabetes or glucose >=200 mg/dL is present with beta-hydroxybutyrate >=3.0 mmol/L or urine ketones >=2+ and metabolic acidosis with pH <7.3 and/or bicarbonate <18 mmol/L; evaluate acidosis even if glucose is <200 mg/dL when SGLT2 inhibitor, pregnancy, fasting, or poor intake raises euglycemic DKA risk.
data_needed: urine ketones if blood ketones unavailable
criteria_options.source_section: Adult DKA diagnosis and euglycemic DKA

criteria_options.label: Adult HHS: glucose >=600 mg/dL with effective osmolality >300 mOsm/kg or total osmolality >320 mOsm/kg and absent significant ketonemia/acidosis
criteria_options.criteria_text: Classify HHS when glucose >=600 mg/dL is accompanied by effective osmolality >300 mOsm/kg or total osmolality >320 mOsm/kg, absent significant ketonemia, and no significant acidosis; assess for mixed DKA/HHS whenever ketones or acidosis coexist.
criteria_options.source_section: Adult HHS diagnosis and mixed crisis

criteria_options.label: Adult DKA/HHS insulin safety: insulin should be delayed when potassium is <3.5 mmol/L and delay insulin until K is >3.5 mmol/L
criteria_options.criteria_text: Before insulin infusion, check potassium and ECG/renal risk; insulin should be delayed when potassium is <3.5 mmol/L, potassium should be replaced with monitored protocol care, and delay insulin until K is >3.5 mmol/L.

criteria_options.label: Adult DKA/HHS initial treatment: isotonic fluid, potassium-safe insulin, dextrose when glucose approaches DKA/HHS targets, and serial labs
criteria_options.criteria_text: Treat DKA/HHS with immediate isotonic crystalloid unless fluid-overload risk changes the plan, potassium-aware insulin therapy after safety labs, bedside glucose every 1-2 h, electrolytes/creatinine/phosphate/beta-hydroxybutyrate/venous pH about every 4 h for DKA, and osmolality about every 4 h for HHS.

criteria_options.label: Adult DKA/HHS transition: biochemical resolution and clinical stability with basal insulin overlap 1-2 h before stopping IV insulin; do not use anion gap alone

guideline_cutoffs.criteria_text: Classify HHS when glucose >=600 mg/dL is accompanied by effective osmolality >300 mOsm/kg or total osmolality >320 mOsm/kg, absent significant ketonemia, and no significant acidosis; assess for mixed DKA/HHS whenever ketones or acidosis coexist.
guideline_cutoffs.source_section: Adult HHS diagnosis and mixed crisis

guideline_cutoffs.label: Adult DKA/HHS insulin safety: insulin should be delayed when potassium is <3.5 mmol/L and delay insulin until K is >3.5 mmol/L
guideline_cutoffs.criteria_text: Before insulin infusion, check potassium and ECG/renal risk; insulin should be delayed when potassium is <3.5 mmol/L, potassium should be replaced with monitored protocol care, and delay insulin until K is >3.5 mmol/L.

guideline_cutoffs.label: Adult DKA/HHS initial treatment: isotonic fluid, potassium-safe insulin, dextrose when glucose approaches DKA/HHS targets, and serial labs
guideline_cutoffs.criteria_text: Treat DKA/HHS with immediate isotonic crystalloid unless fluid-overload risk changes the plan, potassium-aware insulin therapy after safety labs, bedside glucose every 1-2 h, electrolytes/creatinine/phosphate/beta-hydroxybutyrate/venous pH about every 4 h for DKA, and osmolality about every 4 h for HHS.

guideline_cutoffs.label: Adult DKA/HHS transition: biochemical resolution and clinical stability with basal insulin overlap 1-2 h before stopping IV insulin; do not use anion gap alone
guideline_cutoffs.criteria_text: Transition off IV insulin only after biochemical resolution and clinical stability; use basal-bolus insulin with basal overlap 1-2 h before stopping IV insulin and do not use anion gap alone as the resolution criterion.

guideline_cutoffs.label: Adult non-crisis inpatient hyperglycemia: persistent inpatient glucose >=180 mg/dL generally needs insulin initiation or intensification
guideline_cutoffs.criteria_text: When DKA/HHS criteria are absent, persistent inpatient glucose >=180 mg/dL generally warrants insulin initiation or intensification, medication reconciliation, nutrition/insulin matching, and follow-up planning; use A1c if no reliable result in the prior 3 months.

clinical_criteria.criteria_text: Transition off IV insulin only after biochemical resolution and clinical stability; use basal-bolus insulin with basal overlap 1-2 h before stopping IV insulin and do not use anion gap alone as the resolution criterion.

clinical_criteria.label: Adult non-crisis inpatient hyperglycemia: persistent inpatient glucose >=180 mg/dL generally needs insulin initiation or intensification
clinical_criteria.criteria_text: When DKA/HHS criteria are absent, persistent inpatient glucose >=180 mg/dL generally warrants insulin initiation or intensification, medication reconciliation, nutrition/insulin matching, and follow-up planning; use A1c if no reliable result in the prior 3 months.

parallel_actions: full vital signs and mental status
A1c if no reliable result in prior 3 months
required_data: urine ketones if blood ketones unavailable

guideline_cutoffs.label: Adult DKA: diabetes/prior diabetes or glucose >=200 mg/dL, beta-hydroxybutyrate >=3.0 mmol/L or urine ketones >=2+, and pH <7.3 and/or bicarbonate <18 mmol/L
guideline_cutoffs.criteria_text: Classify DKA when diabetes/prior diabetes or glucose >=200 mg/dL is present with beta-hydroxybutyrate >=3.0 mmol/L or urine ketones >=2+ and metabolic acidosis with pH <7.3 and/or bicarbonate <18 mmol/L; evaluate acidosis even if glucose is <200 mg/dL when SGLT2 inhibitor, pregnancy, fasting, or poor intake raises euglycemic DKA risk.
data_needed: urine ketones if blood ketones unavailable
guideline_cutoffs.source_section: Adult DKA diagnosis and euglycemic DKA

guideline_cutoffs.label: Adult HHS: glucose >=600 mg/dL with effective osmolality >300 mOsm/kg or total osmolality >320 mOsm/kg and absent significant ketonemia/acidosis
guideline_cutoffs.criteria_text: Classify HHS when glucose >=600 mg/dL is accompanied by effective osmolality >300 mOsm/kg or total osmolality >320 mOsm/kg, absent significant ketonemia, and no significant acidosis; assess for mixed DKA/HHS whenever ketones or acidosis coexist.
guideline_cutoffs.source_section: Adult HHS diagnosis and mixed crisis

guideline_cutoffs.label: Adult DKA/HHS insulin safety: insulin should be delayed when potassium is <3.5 mmol/L and delay insulin until K is >3.5 mmol/L
guideline_cutoffs.criteria_text: Before insulin infusion, check potassium and ECG/renal risk; insulin should be delayed when potassium is <3.5 mmol/L, potassium should be replaced with monitored protocol care, and delay insulin until K is >3.5 mmol/L.

guideline_cutoffs.label: Adult DKA/HHS initial treatment: isotonic fluid, potassium-safe insulin, dextrose when glucose approaches DKA/HHS targets, and serial labs
guideline_cutoffs.criteria_text: Treat DKA/HHS with immediate isotonic crystalloid unless fluid-overload risk changes the plan, potassium-aware insulin therapy after safety labs, bedside glucose every 1-2 h, electrolytes/creatinine/phosphate/beta-hydroxybutyrate/venous pH about every 4 h for DKA, and osmolality about every 4 h for HHS.

synthetic_patient_scenarios.expected_active_branch: Potassium/ECG/renal hazard: insulin should be delayed when potassium is <3.5 mmol/L, marked hyperkalemia/ECG change is present, or renal failure prevents safe replacement
DKA branch: prior diabetes or glucose >=200 mg/dL, beta-hydroxybutyrate >=3.0 mmol/L or urine ketones >=2+, and pH <7.3 and/or bicarbonate <18 mmol/L
HHS branch: glucose >=600 mg/dL with effective osmolality >300 mOsm/kg or total osmolality >320 mOsm/kg, minimal ketonemia, and no major acidosis
Mixed crisis: DKA ketone/acidosis thresholds coexist with HHS hyperglycemia/osmolality thresholds or altered mentation
HHS branch: glucose >=600 mg/dL with effective osmolality >300 mOsm/kg or total osmolality >320 mOsm/kg, minimal ketonemia, and no major acidosis
HHS response branch: osmolality falling safely, mental status improving, perfusion restored, electrolyte plan stable, precipitant treated, and nutrition/insulin plan ready
No DKA/HHS acidosis, ketone, osmolality, shock, or mental-status branch; persistent inpatient glucose >=180 mg/dL or outpatient hyperglycemia still needs diabetes plan
DKA improving: biochemical resolution, stable vitals/mentation, oral intake possible, precipitant owned, and basal insulin can overlap IV insulin 1-2 h
Ketotic acidosis even if glucose is <200 mg/dL, or pregnancy/frailty/heart failure/advanced kidney disease/SGLT2 exposure changes standard fluid or insulin assumptions

synthetic_context.branch_trigger: Ketotic acidosis even if glucose is <200 mg/dL, or pregnancy/frailty/heart failure/advanced kidney disease/SGLT2 exposure changes standard fluid or insulin assumptions

synthetic_patient_scenarios.expected_active_branch: DKA/HHS thresholds absent or another cause better explains symptoms: starvation/alcohol ketosis, lactic acidosis, renal failure, intoxication, sepsis, pancreatitis, MI/stroke, medication effect, or lab artifact

synthetic_context.branch_trigger: DKA/HHS thresholds absent or another cause better explains symptoms: starvation/alcohol ketosis, lactic acidosis, renal failure, intoxication, sepsis, pancreatitis, MI/stroke, medication effect, or lab artifact

hand_polished_requirements: adult DKA branch uses glucose, ketone, pH, bicarbonate, potassium, and transition thresholds
adult HHS branch uses glucose and osmolality thresholds with neurologic monitoring
mixed DKA/HHS and euglycemic/SGLT2/pregnancy branches do not fall through routine hyperglycemia
potassium <3.5 mmol/L blocks insulin until corrected
local insulin infusion, electrolyte, ICU, and SGLT2 restart policies remain clinician-governed
